name: Hoax.ai Interface Grammar
governing_principle: The interface must embody evidence posture, not simulate detection authority. A Hoax.ai interface may show structure, posture, uncertainty, source conditions, and boundaries; it must not visually imply verdict, certainty, accusation, or automated detection.
accessibility_rules: Use semantic HTML for all core meaning.
Core thesis must be readable as plain text without JavaScript.
Text must remain legible on mobile viewports.
No core meaning may be hidden in decorative visuals alone.
Use headings, lists, and landmarks for structure.
Maintain sufficient color contrast for body text.
performance_rules: Static-first delivery without required client frameworks.
No external library required for core thesis reading.
Avoid heavy assets on thesis surface.
No WebGL or canvas required for core meaning.
motion_rules: Motion must explain evidence layers or uncertainty only.
Interface must remain understandable with motion disabled.
dependency_rules: No external JavaScript libraries on thesis surface without explicit future decision approval.
No external CSS frameworks without explicit future decision approval.
External libraries require explicit approval in a future decision before use.
No WebGL or canvas for core thesis communication.
